Package simulation

Interface SimulationScenario

All Known Implementing Classes:
WSNDataCollectionScenario
public interface SimulationScenario
Composition root for a simulation experiment.

Implement this interface to define a complete, self-contained experiment: field configuration, target movement model, UAV strategies to compare, and the path-planning algorithm to use.

Quickstart — implement three methods

   public class MyScenario implements SimulationScenario {

       @Override
       public String getName() { return "My Experiment"; }

       @Override
       public SimulationConfig getConfig() {
           return SimulationConfig.builder()
               .fieldSize(3000, 3000)
               .nodeCount(100)
               .duration(8000L)
               .build();
       }

       @Override
       public List<UAVIntelligence> getIntelligences() {
           return Arrays.asList(
               new PredefinedPatrolIntelligence(),
               new MyCustomIntelligence(getConfig()));
       }
   }

Running a scenario

   SimulationRunner runner = SimulationRunner.fromScenario(new MyScenario())
       .withSeed(42L);

   runner.run();                              // headless, sequential
   runner.runInParallel(listeners, callback); // side-by-side GUI

Extension points

Override getPlannerFactory() to plug in a path-planning algorithm, and getMovementStrategy() to change how targets move. Both have sensible defaults so you only override what your scenario needs.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    SimulationConfig
    getConfig()
    Field dimensions, timing, drone range, and node parameters for this scenario.
    default NodeDeploymentStrategy
    getDeploymentStrategy()
    Strategy controlling how targets are spatially deployed at the start of each simulation pass.
    default String
    getDescription()
    Optional one-line description for documentation / README purposes.
    List<UAVIntelligence>
    getIntelligences()
    Ordered list of UAV intelligence strategies to compare in this scenario.
    default MovementStrategy
    getMovementStrategy()
    Movement model applied to all targets (sensor nodes, drones, etc.) throughout the simulation.
    String
    getName()
    Human-readable name shown in run reports and the GUI header.
    default Supplier<PathPlanner>
    getPlannerFactory()
    Factory called once per simulation pass to produce a fresh PathPlanner.

  • Method Details

    • getName

      String getName()
      Human-readable name shown in run reports and the GUI header.

    • getDescription

      default String getDescription()
      Optional one-line description for documentation / README purposes.

    • getConfig

      SimulationConfig getConfig()
      Field dimensions, timing, drone range, and node parameters for this scenario. Use SimulationConfig.builder() to construct.

    • getIntelligences

      List<UAVIntelligence> getIntelligences()
      Ordered list of UAV intelligence strategies to compare in this scenario. The first entry is treated as the baseline in comparison reports.

      Each call to this method should return fresh instances because intelligences are stateful (they own coverage grids, knowledge bases, etc.).

    • getPlannerFactory

      default Supplier<PathPlanner> getPlannerFactory()
      Factory called once per simulation pass to produce a fresh PathPlanner. Using a factory (rather than a shared instance) is important when passes run in parallel.

      Defaults to a pass-through planner that returns the provided target list as-is (no route optimisation). Override to plug in a custom planning algorithm without touching any runner or UAV code: 

         @Override
   public Supplier<PathPlanner> getPlannerFactory() {
       GaAcoConfig cfg = GaAcoConfig.defaults();
       return () -> new GaAcoPlanner(cfg);
   }

    • getMovementStrategy

      default MovementStrategy getMovementStrategy()
      Movement model applied to all targets (sensor nodes, drones, etc.) throughout the simulation. A new instance is created for each simulation pass via this method.

      Defaults to a Lévy-walk / Markov-chain model matching the original thesis scenario. Override to model faster targets, directed movement, ballistic trajectories, or any other mobility pattern.

    • getDeploymentStrategy

      default NodeDeploymentStrategy getDeploymentStrategy()
      Strategy controlling how targets are spatially deployed at the start of each simulation pass.

      Defaults to NodeDeploymentStrategy.stratified() — stratified random sampling — which divides the field into a uniform grid and places exactly one node per cell. This guarantees spatial uniformity regardless of the random seed, removing the need to cherry-pick seeds and making results comparable across runs.

      Override with NodeDeploymentStrategy.random() to reproduce the original fully-random placement, or supply a custom strategy for non-uniform scenarios (e.g. clustered deployment near disaster sites).
